result.content: The Influence of Sleep Deprivation Medically on the Overall Wellbeing of People Living in the United States  AP Seminar  January 27, 2025 Word Count: 1294        Introduction The exiguity of sleep better known as sleep deprivation is a root cause for many long-lasting illnesses and medical conditions. The decrease in the prioritization of a healthy and sustainable sleep schedule is prevalent in America. Many Americans fail to maintain a healthy sleep schedule. Due to lifestyle shifts and a change in attitude towards sleep, people are continuing their terrible sleeping habits. Many people fail to realize the harm they are causing themselves. “Many people are unaware of the potential adverse health effects of habitual sleep restriction” (Alvarez, et al., 2007). Published by the reputable Wiley Online Library, this study underscores the serious health consequences of sleep deprivation. Sleep deprivation could foster the worsening of our physical health, mood and cognitive abilities. “The prevalence of physical distress, mental distress, activity limitation, was significantly higher than that in the healthy group” (Liu, et al., 2018). The authors of this article conducted a study with two groups, one group had sufficient sleep, while the other was sleep-deprived. The article was peer-reviewed, and the authors are all experts on this topic. If not made a primary concern, sleep deprivation has significant impacts on the physical health, cognitive abilities, and mood of people residing throughout the United States of America. The Impact of Sleep Deprivation on Physical Health As previously mentioned, while sleep deficit has been identified as one of the root causes of the deterioration of human health, sleep holds significant influence particularly over the physical well-being of humans. Sleep is essential for daily physical activity. The continuous lack of good sleep will lead to huge problems relating to the physical health of humans. “We have postulated a number of physiologic relationships between the behaviors of sleep and exercise” (Atkinson, et al., 2007). This article dives deeper into the relationships between sleep, physical health, and human health. The authors are part of research institutes of sports and exercise science showing their expertise thus illustrating the article’s credibility. It elaborates on the correlation between physical health, performance, and sleep quality. Sleep affects bodily functions like our immune systems, metabolism, muscle recovery, and growth. “The bottom line, is that there is no substitute for getting good quality sleep if we want to reap the immune system benefits” (Yale Medicine, 2023). This article has been peer-reviewed and is credible. It aims to establish the connection between the immune system and increased sleep duration deeming it relevant to the topic. Athletes and trainers prioritize sleep because it enhances physical performance. Sports analysts have noticed tremendous differences in the performance of athletes solely based on their sleep duration as well as other attributes such as their hand-eye coordination, reaction time, and endurance. In addition to this, sleeplessness has been seen to cause heart inflammation. “What we are learning is that sleep modulates the production of cells that are the protagonists of inflammation” (Filip et al., 2022). This article was published by the National Institution of Health a reputed research organization. Furthermore, a lack of sleep can also cause us to be susceptible to severe cardiovascular problems. “Reports from studies document associations between sleep duration and various health problems such as cardiovascular events, risk of stroke, incident artery calcification, changes in inflammatory markers, and many more” (Buysse et al., 2010). This article in particular tries to evaluate whether an improvement in sleep can also improve health. The article has been peer-reviewed and is also published by ScienceDirect a reputed research organization. All the pieces of evidence used connect because they all show the extent to which sleep deficiency can impact the physical health of humans. All in all, this demonstrates the major degree to which sleep deprivation can impact the physical aspects of human health due to its impact on physical performance, bodily functions, the immune system and ability to cause cardiovascular problems. The Impact of Sleep Deprivation on the Cognitive Abilities of Humans Another key point is the negative ramifications caused by sleep dept on the cognitive functioning of humans. Cognitive functioning refer to the brain’s ability to process information and apply it to solve problems. When talking about cognitive abilities important traits include intellectual capacity, critical thinking and memorization ability. “Many studies have documented the effects of sleep loss on aspects of cognitive functioning” (Grandner, 2018). The article was published by a reputable author with a doctorate who is a c
